website.footerHeading: Ready for your next legal question?

website.disclaimer: Alma Lex does not provide legal advice. For specific cases, consult a qualified professional.

jsonLd.description: Free Swiss legal AI. Answers legal questions about the Code of Obligations (OR), Civil Code (ZGB), Criminal Code (StGB) and Federal Court decisions with source references.

hero.subtitle: Ask your legal question about the Code of Obligations (OR), Civil Code (ZGB) or Federal Court decisions and get a source-backed answer in seconds. Free and without registration.

chatPreview.userMessage: What is the notice period for a residential lease?
chatPreview.botResponse: According to Art. 266c OR, the notice period for residential leases is at least three months, effective on a locally customary date...

dataSources.title: Built on official legal sources
dataSources.subtitle: Alma Lex uses exclusively official Swiss legal sources. The same ones used at universities and law schools.

federal.title: Federal Law
federal.subtitle: Classified Compilation of Federal Legislation
federal.desc: Code of Obligations (OR), Civil Code (ZGB), Criminal Code (StGB) and other federal statutes from the official Classified Compilation. Fully searchable and always up to date.

court.title: Federal Court Decisions
court.subtitle: Leading Cases & Rulings
court.desc: Leading cases and rulings of the Swiss Federal Court, searchable by area of law, topic, and legal provision.

privacy.title: Your data belongs to you.
privacy.subtitle: No tracking, no cookies, no hidden costs. Privacy is not a feature, it is a core principle.

cards.title: No data stored
cards.desc: Your chat histories stay locally in your browser. Nothing is stored on our servers.

cards.title: AI hosted in Geneva
cards.desc: The AI models run at Infomaniak, a Swiss cloud provider with a datacenter in Geneva.

cards.title: Server in Europe
cards.desc: The application runs on a dedicated server in Finland. No US cloud, all data stays in Europe.

cards2.title: No AI training
cards2.desc: Your inputs are never used to train AI models.

cards2.title: No login needed
cards2.desc: Just get started. No account, no email, no personal data required.

items.a: Alma Lex is a free Swiss legal AI. It searches federal law and Federal Court decisions to answer your legal questions clearly and with source references. No login required.

items.q: How do I start a chat?
items.a: Simply type your question into the input field and press Enter. Alma Lex will respond immediately. No account or login needed.

items.q: What kind of questions can I ask?
items.a: You can ask questions about any area of Swiss law, such as the Code of Obligations (OR), Civil Code (ZGB), Criminal Code (StGB), tenancy law, employment law, or family law. Alma Lex searches federal statutes and thousands of Federal Court decisions.

items.q: Where does the information come from?
items.a: Alma Lex uses exclusively official Swiss legal sources: the Classified Compilation of Federal Legislation (fedlex.admin.ch) and Federal Court decisions (bger.ch). The same sources used at universities and law schools.

Every answer includes references to the relevant legal provisions and court rulings. Alma Lex provides a solid first orientation but does not replace professional legal advice. For specific legal matters, always consult a qualified professional.

Yes. Chat histories stay locally in your browser. The AI models run at Infomaniak, a Swiss cloud provider with a datacenter in Geneva. The application itself runs on a server in Finland. Your inputs are neither stored nor used for AI training.
Alma Lex is completely free. No hidden costs, no subscription, no ads.
Yes, the complete source code is publicly available on GitHub. Every line of code, from the AI pipeline to the frontend, can be inspected.

Alma Lex does not replace professional legal advice. The answers provide a first orientation but may not account for all case-specific nuances. For specific legal matters, you should consult a qualified professional.

items.q: In which languages can I ask questions?
items.a: You can ask your questions in English. The legal sources are available in German. Additional language support is planned.

openSource.title: Fully open source.
openSource.subtitle: Alma Lex is a free, open-source legal AI from Switzerland. Every line of code is publicly available on GitHub.

cards.title: Swiss Legal Database
cards.desc: Built on Swiss federal law and Federal Court decisions. The AI searches current sources before every answer.

cards.title: Open-Source AI Models
cards.desc: Multiple models to choose from: GPT-OSS 120B, Qwen3, Llama 3.3, Apertus 70B, and Mistral Small 3.2.

cards.title: Precise Legal Search
cards.desc: BGE-Multilingual-Gemma2 for semantic search. BGE-Reranker-v2-m3 for precise ranking of search results.

cards.title: Source Code on GitHub
cards.desc: Complete source code publicly available. Contributions, issues, and feedback welcome.

cards.title: AI Hosted in Switzerland
cards.desc: All AI models run at Infomaniak in Geneva. No data leaves Switzerland for processing.

cards.title: Independent of Big Tech
cards.desc: No OpenAI, no Google, no Microsoft. Exclusively open models and self-hosted infrastructure.
